steps.title: Recognize similar triangles and write the proportion
steps.content:
When the sun creates shadows at the same time of day, the sun's angle is the same for both objects. The Washington Monument, its shadow, and the sun's rays form a triangle similar to the triangle formed by the cherry tree, its shadow, and the sun's rays. For similar triangles, the ratio of height to shadow length must be equal:

$$\frac{\text{Monument height}}{\text{Monument shadow}} = \frac{\text{Tree height}}{\text{Tree shadow}}$$

$$\frac{555}{300} = \frac{h}{16}$$

steps.title: Solve for the tree's height
steps.content:
Cross multiply to solve for $h$:

$$555 \times 16 = 300h$$

$$8880 = 300h$$

$$h = \frac{8880}{300} = 29.6 \text{ feet}$$

The value 29.6 feet is closest to 30 feet. That matches choice C — C is correct.
